concerns.text: Joint regression with crossing number and braid index as separate predictors violates independence assumption (braid index ≤ crossing number by definition). Spec acknowledges this in FR-005 but still requires fitting joint models without clarifying that coefficients are NOT interpretable as independent effects. This redefines the research question from 'jointly explain variation' to 'describe variance partitioning within census' without explicit scope boundary.

Validation against KnotInfo is circular—both databases derive from same underlying knot tables (Hoste-Thistlethwaite-Weeks enumeration). Spec acknowledges this in FR-013 but still requires ≥90% match threshold as validation pass/fail. This provides no genuine evidence of measurement accuracy and misrepresents validation scope.

User Story 2 (Establish Measurement Precision for Core Invariants) is not linked to any functional requirement or success criterion. SC-008 covers core-invariant coverage but no FR explicitly requires precision-threshold validation. This leaves the story without concrete implementation guidance.

FR-004 (exploratory EDA with scatter plots of crossing number vs. braid index, stratified by alternating classification) is not referenced in any success criterion or implementation guidance. This creates a gap between requirement and verification.

KnotRecord entity definition omits required top-level fields source_timestamp and checksum_sha256 that the knot_record.schema.yaml contract mandates. This mismatch will cause schema validation failures.

Spec references Knot Atlas dataset but does not provide formal verified dataset citation (Verified Datasets block with canonical URL https://katlas.org). Without this citation the dataset cannot be validated by the deterministic resolver.

Multiple arXiv citations in Assumptions section have placeholder identifiers (e.g., 'https://arxiv.org/abs/math/[identifier]') that are not resolvable. These should be replaced with actual arXiv IDs or removed.
